note: Empirical frequencies from the regenerated state-episode panel. Rates are computed over NOTABLE episodes (Storm Events property damage > 0). No probabilities, no fitted model. PA is obligated, IHP approved.

provenance.unit: state-storm episode — each NOAA Storm Events episode's counties aggregated to the state level
provenance.hazardMembership: an episode counts toward a hazard if it carried that hazard's signal (flood: a county over flood stage or a Flood/Winter incident; tornado: ≥1 tornado; wind: gust ≥50 mph; hail: ≥0.75 in; winter: snowfall present)
provenance.declaredRule: declared = at least one designated county of the episode falls inside a FEMA disaster window (OpenFEMA); the bar gets cleared at the STATE level, not per county
provenance.denominator: NOTABLE episodes only — Storm Events property damage > 0; this strips the many trivial $0 NWS reports that flatten the curve
provenance.metric: each driver value is the PEAK across the episode's counties (peak envelope)
provenance.caveat: Storm Events damage under-records riverine/agricultural flood loss, so the damage>0 denominator excludes some real flood declarations (counted per state as floodExcluded)
provenance.source: scripts/build_context.py over data/state_panel.json (NOAA Storm Events + OpenFEMA)

denominator.floodCaveat: 92 declared flood episodes here have $0 recorded Storm Events damage (riverine/agricultural loss isn't in that field) and are excluded from these rates — so flood is undercounted by this filter.

flood.label: Flooding
flood.caveat: Rainfall separates declared floods in WI/IL/IN/OH/MI, but NOT in MN — Minnesota floods are spring snowmelt, so watch snowpack/melt and river crest there.

rainEventARIyr.label: rainfall rarity (Atlas-14 recurrence)
rainEventARIyr.basis: NOAA Atlas-14 recurrence interval of the event rainfall (county max)

maxFtAboveFlood.label: river crest above flood stage
maxFtAboveFlood.basis: USGS observed crest minus NWS flood stage (gaged counties)

maxSweMeltIn.label: snowmelt water released (est.)
maxSweMeltIn.basis: ESTIMATE: observed 1-day snow-depth drop × 0.30 settled-snowpack density

tornado.label: Tornado
tornado.caveat: Tornado COUNT and EF barely separate declared from non-declared episodes — footprint and property damage are the real separators (see the footprint panels).

wind.label: Wind
wind.caveat: Peak gust is recorded well, but declarations track damage/extent more than the gust value.

hail.label: Hail
hail.caveat: Max hail size is recorded well, but declarations track damage/extent more than the stone size.

winter.label: Winter storm
winter.caveat: Thin sample (few winter-only declarations); snowfall is real, snowpack water (SWE) is an estimate from observed depth.

maxSweAntecedentIn.label: antecedent snowpack water (est.)
maxSweAntecedentIn.basis: ESTIMATE: observed antecedent snow depth × 0.30 settled-snowpack density

costSummary.source: declared state-episodes + OpenFEMA (PA obligated / IHP approved)
